response.justification: Candidate has 15+ years of strong hands-on software engineering experience across diverse stacks, with demonstrated technical depth, open-source leadership, and community teaching (workshops, courses, dojos). However, the role is a people-management position requiring 2+ years of formal people management and 3+ years building developer tools (compilers, build systems, test automation) at scale. The resume shows no explicit people-management or direct-reports experience, and while there is some developer-tooling work (CI/CD setup, test frameworks like Trial/Fluent-Asserts, MCP tooling), it is not the primary focus of recent roles. Mobile platform ecosystem experience is also limited. Worth a screening call to probe leadership scope and tooling depth before deciding.
strengths: Strong polyglot engineering background with 15+ years across web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Demonstrated developer-tooling contributions: test runner (Trial), assertion library (Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD setup, MCP code generators, and TDD/trunk-based development advocacy
Proven knowledge-sharing and mentoring through workshops, Selenium courses, Refactoring Dojos, and conference talks — adjacent to leadership
concerns: No explicit people-management or direct-reports experience listed; all roles are individual-contributor titles (Senior/Staff Software Developer)
Limited evidence of mobile-platform ecosystem work at scale (some iOS/Apple TV app maintenance, but not platform-infrastructure)
Developer-tools experience is present but scattered across side projects rather than 3+ years owning build systems, compilers, or test automation as a primary role

key_factors.factor: Partial fit on developer-tooling and testing infrastructure focus
